news.headline: Stocks plummeted in the second worst day of the year amid tariff concerns.
Generation Mining files technical report for Marathon Copper-Palladium Project, with an effective date of Nov. 1, 2024.
news.summary: In Toronto, Generation Mining Limited (TSX: GENM; OTCQB: GENMF) has released a National Instrument 43-101 Technical Report titled “Marathon Copper-Palladium Project - Feasibility Study Report Update" on March 28, 2025. The report, effective as of November 1, 2024, was accompanied by a news announcement on March 27, 2025. Interested individuals can access the report for download from the Company’s website at www.genmining.com.

news.headline: Trump commutes sentence of Ozy Media founder who faced almost 10 years for fraud, just before surrender.
news.summary: Carlos Watson, the founder of a failed digital media firm, has been handed a sentence of nearly a decade in prison. He was found guilty of attempting to deceive investors and lenders.

news.headline: CZ pledges 500 BNB for Thailand, Myanmar disaster relief after 7.7 magnitude earthquake. 144 dead, 732 injured, relief ongoing. #CryptoAid
news.summary: Binance co-founder Changpeng “CZ” Zhao is donating 500 BNB each to Thailand and Myanmar after a 7.7 magnitude earthquake. The funds will be distributed via Binance if a donation platform is not found. At least 144 people have died, and 732 are injured in the disaster. Myanmar’s junta chief is seeking international aid. Cryptocurrencies like BNB offer efficient, low-cost, and fast ways to provide disaster relief. Past examples include a Turkish-Syrian quake and Maui wildfires fundraisers, raising over $1 million each. Company The Giving Block raised $1 million for the California wildfire relief fund. Crypto is revolutionizing how aid is delivered.

news.headline: New AI wave with MCP; AI agents can do tasks triggered by prompts. SGNL ensures responsible use.
news.summary: A new development in AI-powered automation is emerging in the business sector. Agents utilizing large language models are now equipped to carry out various tasks within internal systems, including updating records, analyzing data, and executing actions upon receiving a basic command. However, this advancement also poses potential risks, as unchecked agents may gain unauthorized access to sensitive information. Today, SGNL, a contemporary privileged identity management (PIM) platform, revealed a solution to mitigate these risks.

news.headline: FDIC changes rules for banks, no pre-approval needed for crypto services. US regulation sees major shift.
news.summary: A major change in U.S. regulations has occurred as the Federal agency has decided to reverse the policy that mandated banks to seek approval before providing cryptocurrency services. This move signals a significant shift in the regulatory environment for cryptocurrencies in the country. This new development means that banks will now have more freedom to offer cryptocurrency services without the previous requirement for permission. This decision is set to impact a large number of financial institutions and the vast cryptocurrency market in the U.S. as they adapt to this new regulatory landscape.

news.headline: Eric Ruark from NumbersUSA to discuss immigration weaponization at panel hosted by Center for Immigration Studies in Arlington, VA on March 28, 2025.
news.summary: On March 28, 2025, in Arlington, Virginia, the Center for Immigration Studies will organize a panel event to explore the multifaceted use of immigration as a tool by various entities globally. The discussion will delve into the political, economic, and strategic implications of immigration, including its role in mass migration crises.

news.headline: FCPT buys 6 Whataburger properties from MWB Restaurants for $16.8 million in a sale-leaseback deal.
news.summary: A real estate investment trust, Four Corners Property Trust (NYSE:FCPT), has acquired six Whataburger properties from MWB Restaurants for $16.8 million through a sale-leaseback transaction. These properties are recently built and situated in robust retail areas in Alabama. The Whataburger locations are operated by franchisees under long-term leases.

news.headline: Elon Musk integrates X with his AI vision, big move in tech world with huge impact.
Congresswoman Deborah Ross meets with NCICU colleges in Raleigh, discusses higher education.
news.summary: Last week in downtown Raleigh, North Carolina, a roundtable was organized by the North Carolina Independent Colleges and Universities (NCICU) with U.S. Congresswoman Deborah Ross from North Carolina's 2nd Congressional District. Ross serves as the Co-Chair of the Congressional Independent Colleges Caucus (CICC). The event facilitated discussions on pertinent issues and was attended by a notable number of participants.

news.headline: VIQ Solutions reports $6.0 million adjusted EBITDA improvement in FY 2024 due to AI automation and customer renewals.
news.summary: VIQ Solutions Inc., based in Mississauga, Ontario, a leading company in secure, AI-driven voice and video capture technology and transcription services, revealed its unaudited financial results for the fourth quarter and fiscal year ending December 31, 2024. The company, listed as VQS on the Toronto Stock Exchange (TSX), reported its figures in U.S. dollars, following International Financial Reporting Standards (IFRS). VIQ demonstrated a notable $6.0 million increase in Adjusted EBITDA compared to the previous year.

news.headline: Callan JMB posted FY2024 results and update: IPO closed at $4/share on Feb 6, 2025, raising ~$5.7M, incl partial over-allotment.
news.summary: On February 6, 2025, an initial public offering (IPO) was completed, with shares priced at $4.00 each. The IPO generated gross proceeds of about $5.7 million. This total includes the partial exercise of the over-allotment option.

news.headline: Coherent Corp. launches industry-first 100G ZR QSFP28-DCO with 0dBm optical output power for metro and regional ROADM systems.
news.summary: In SAXONBURG, Pennsylvania, on March 28, 2025, Coherent Corp. revealed the launch of the pioneering 100G ZR QSFP28-DCO for the photonics sector. The product, now widely accessible, boasts 0dBm optical output power, specifically crafted for deployment in metro and regional ROADM-based line systems. This development positions Coherent Corp. (NYSE: COHR) as a frontrunner in the industry for cutting-edge photonics technology.

news.headline: GOGL appoints Carl Erik Steen & James Ayers as Directors. (Characters: 62)
news.summary: Golden Ocean Group Limited (listed on Nasdaq as GOGL and OSE as GOGL) has recently appointed Mr. Carl Erik Steen and Mr. James Ayers as Directors of the Company.

news.headline: Knight Therapeutics Inc. will be listed on The Globe and Mail's 2025 Women Lead Here list for executive gender diversity.
news.summary: Montreal, March 28, 2025 - Knight Therapeutics Inc. (TSX: GUD) is set to be acknowledged on The Globe and Mail's 2025 Report on Business magazine's sixth annual Women Lead Here list, showcasing top Canadian businesses excelling in executive gender diversity. This recognition marks a significant achievement for Knight as it highlights the company's dedication to promoting gender equality within its leadership.

news.headline: FBI investigating Oracle cyberattack
NASA awards SpaceX the launch service contract for Starship, adding to Falcon 9 and Falcon Heavy.
news.summary: In a recent announcement on March 28, 2025, it was disclosed that NASA has granted a modification to SpaceX, based in Starbase, Texas, under the NASA Launch Services (NLS) II contract. This modification allows SpaceX to include Starship in addition to their current launch services of Falcon 9 and Falcon Heavy. The NLS II contracts offer a wide array of services, and this modification serves to enhance SpaceX's capabilities by incorporating Starship into their existing offerings.

news.headline: Senators urge regulators to consider conflicts of Trump-backed WLFI stablecoin, USD1. Legislation proposed. $550M raised.March 28 letter sent to OCC, Fed. Trumf fam controls 60% equity in WLFI. Trump Jr. promotes USD1 coin.
news.summary: Five Democratic lawmakers in the US Senate have raised concerns about the potential conflicts of interest stemming from a stablecoin launched by World Liberty Financial (WLFI), a crypto firm backed by US President Donald Trump's family. In a letter dated March 28, Senators, including Elizabeth Warren, asked regulatory agency leaders about their plans to oversee WLFI and its stablecoin, USD1. The lawmakers are also considering the GENIUS Act, which would empower the OCC and Federal Reserve to regulate stablecoins. Trump's involvement in WLFI, coupled with his executive order for agencies to collaborate with the White House, raises conflict of interest and financial system integrity concerns. Since its launch in September 2024, WLFI has raised $550 million through token sales, with Trump and family controlling a significant portion of the company. The recent launch of USD1 has further fueled debates over potential conflicts of interest and regulatory oversight in the crypto space.

news.headline: Blackstone may invest in TikTok as it prepares to separate from ByteDance, with a deadline looming next week.
news.summary: A prominent private equity firm is contemplating investing in the video app, which is in the process of complying with a legal mandate to sever ties with its Chinese parent company, ByteDance, within the next seven days.

news.headline: Denison Mines Corp files 2024 Annual Report on Form 40-F with SEC; listed on TSX: DML and NYSE American: DNN.
news.summary: Denison Mines Corp. announced the submission of its 2024 Annual Report on Form 40-F to the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission. The company's report is now available, providing stakeholders and investors with essential information. Denison Mines Corp., listed on the Toronto Stock Exchange as DML and NYSE American as DNN, continues to make strides in transparency and compliance. This move signifies Denison's commitment to keeping investors informed and maintaining regulatory standards.

news.headline: August Schools launches first AI agent, August Intelligence, to aid K-12 schools with critical insights, admin tasks, and student support.
news.summary: In New York, August Schools recently introduced August Intelligence, an innovative AI agent to aid schools in managing student wellbeing. This platform aims to provide valuable insights, simplify administrative duties, and improve student assistance. Designed to cater to various school personnel like administrators and counselors, August Intelligence allows users to inquire about their student body and promptly gathers and delivers data-driven responses. This advanced AI system promises to revolutionize school operations and enhance student care.

news.headline: GigaMedia reports $3.0 million in revenue, $1.5 million gross profit for full year 2024. Operating loss disclosed.
news.summary: Today, on March 28, 2025, GigaMedia Limited (NASDAQ: GIGM) disclosed its unaudited financial outcomes for both the final quarter and entirety of 2024. Throughout 2024, GigaMedia documented revenues totaling $3.0 million, alongside a gross profit amounting to $1.5 million. The company also reported an operating loss...

news.headline: Bitcoin price hits $83,387 as 'whales go wild'. S&P 500 plunges 112 points; Wall Street closes 700 points lower. BTC may fall to $65K.
news.summary: Bitcoin price continued to drop on March 28, hitting a low of $83,387 amidst a Wall Street sell-off that saw the DOW drop 700 points and the S&P 500 index fall 112 points. Investor concerns about inflation rose after the February core PCE index showed a 2.8% rise. The markets reacted to President Trump's new tariffs, including the 25% tariff on foreign cars. Analysts predict Bitcoin could reach $65,635 due to a bear wedge pattern. Despite technical indicators showing no oversold conditions, a surge in spot market demand by whales was noted on Bitfinex. Regulatory advancements, including FDIC's guidelines on banks engaging in crypto activities, have boosted the industry. Bitcoin's price outlook remains uncertain pending Trump's upcoming tariff announcements on April 2.

news.headline: Elon Musk's xAI buys his social site in stock deal valued at $33 billion, as announced by the Tesla CEO.
news.summary: Elon Musk, the CEO of Tesla, announced that his artificial intelligence company xAI has acquired his social media platform X in a stock agreement, valuing the social site at $33 billion. This deal marks a significant milestone for Musk's ventures in the tech industry. The acquisition highlights the growing interest and investment in artificial intelligence technologies, with xAI emerging as a major player in the market. The acquisition is seen as a strategic move by Musk to integrate AI capabilities into his social media platform, expanding its reach and capabilities. The deal underscores Musk's vision for the future of technology and his commitment to innovation in various sectors.

news.headline: Innovative Industrial Properties reports defaults by tenants, embarks on tenant replacement and renewal for enhancing performance.
news.summary: In a recent announcement, Innovative Industrial Properties, Inc. (IIP) revealed a strategic move aimed at boosting its real estate portfolio within the regulated cannabis sector. The initiative, which includes tenant replacement and renewal efforts, seeks to improve the performance of its properties and increase shareholder value. IIP, a prominent real estate investment trust listed on the NYSE under the ticker symbol IIPR, is concentrating on enhancing tenant creditworthiness and maximizing property occupancy to adapt to market changes.

news.headline: Mulvihill Premium Yield Fund reports $1.99 million increase in net assets for Class I units in 2024, pays $0.66 distribution.
news.summary: Mulvihill Premium Yield Fund (TSX:MPY) reported its financial results for the year ending December 31, 2024. Net asset increases for various unit classes were: $1.99 million for Class I units ($10.60 per unit), $1.91 million for Class F units ($10.61 per unit), $1.34 million for Class A units ($9.92 per unit), and $1.93 million for ETF units ($9.95 per unit). Distributions of $0.66 per unit were paid to each class of units. The fund aims to provide high quarterly income, long-term capital appreciation, and reduced portfolio volatility through investing in equity securities from indices like S&P/TSX Composite Index and S&P 500 Index, as well as employing option writing strategies for tax-efficient yields. The fund may also invest in other Mulvihill Funds and ETFs, limited to 15% of net asset value.

news.headline: PRG appoints Carl Vogel as Interim CEO to drive growth. Richard Porter steps down. NY, March 28, 2025.
news.summary: Carl Vogel has been appointed as the Interim CEO of PRG (Production Resource Group) with a primary focus on driving growth. The announcement was made on March 28, 2025, in New York. PRG is a global leader in providing production solutions for entertainment and live events. This change comes as Richard Porter steps down from his position. PRG aims to capitalize on Vogel's leadership to enhance its performance and accelerate expansion in the industry.

news.headline: Holiday Inn Express in Pell City, Alabama reopens after full renovation on March 24th, 2025.
news.summary: On March 24th, 2025, the Holiday Inn Express in Pell City, Alabama reached a noteworthy achievement by hosting its grand reopening following a complete renovation. The revitalized hotel, boasting a modernized look, opened its doors to the public and visitors.

news.headline: Mulvihill Enhanced Split Preferred Share ETF reports $0.13M decrease in net assets, totaling $7.41M as of Dec 31 2024, with $0.58M cash distributions to unitholders.
news.summary: Toronto, March 28, 2025 - Mulvihill Enhanced Split Preferred Share ETF (TSX: SPFD) has released its financial results for the year ending December 31, 2024. There was a decrease in net assets for unit holders by $0.13 million, equating to $0.16 per unit. As of December 31, 2024, net assets attributable to holders of units stood at $7.41 million, equivalent to $9.85 per unit. Throughout the year, cash distributions to unitholders amounted to $0.58 million, or $0.72 per unit.

news.headline: Judge blocks Trump from dismantling Voice of America. Journalists allege First Amendment violation.
news.summary: Journalists from Voice of America filed a lawsuit claiming that the actions of the administration had infringed upon their rights protected by the First Amendment.

news.headline: MiRus introduces Siegel™ 8-Fr TAVR with two successful cases in US multi-center EFS, led by Dr. Yadav.
news.summary: MiRus has initiated the Early Feasibility Study for the Siegel™ 8-Fr aortic transcatheter heart valve in the US, conducted across multiple centers. The study began with two successful cases performed by Dr. Pradeep K. Yadav MD this week.

news.headline: Sheriff in Tennessee probes theft of 80,000 lbs of beef loaded onto trucks but never delivered from slaughterhouse.
news.summary: Authorities in Grainger County, Tennessee, are currently looking into a case of theft involving shipments from a meat processing facility. The sheriff’s office has reported that the items were loaded onto trucks but failed to arrive at their intended locations. The exact number of shipments and the value of the stolen goods are currently under investigation.

news.headline: Elon Musk's xAI company buys X for $33 billion, he confirms reported.
Rupert Resources releases audited financial results for the ten months ending Dec 31, 2024. Toronto, March 28, 2025.
news.summary: Toronto, March 28, 2025 - Rupert Resources Ltd, a company based in Toronto, has released its audited financial report for the ten-month period concluding on December 31, 2024. The report includes the Management's Discussion and Analysis for the same period. This update provides stakeholders and investors with key insights into the company's financial performance over this period.

news.headline: Condado Tacos offers half-off burritos and free delivery for the first time, celebrating National Burrito Day.
news.summary: In Columbus, Ohio, on March 28, 2025, Condado Tacos is gearing up for a sizzling celebration on National Burrito Day. Making a notable entry into the burrito scene, the restaurant is offering a special deal: every burrito will be available at a discount of 50% on Thursday, April 3. This promotion is a first-time occurrence for Condado, promising customers an exciting and flavorful experience at a fraction of the usual cost.

news.headline: Premium Global Income Split Corp. reports $0.30 million increase in net assets, $7.52 million total; $0.64 per Preferred share cash distribution in 2024.
news.summary: Premium Global Income Split Corp. (TSX: PGIC; PGIC.PR.A) based in Toronto released its financial results for the year ending on December 31, 2024. The net assets attributable to Class A shareholders saw an increase of $0.30 million, translating to $0.36 per Class A share. As of the end of the year, the net assets for Class A shareholders stood at $7.52 million, equivalent to $7.31 per Class A share. Throughout the year, cash distributions of $0.64 per Preferred share and $0.48 per Class A share were disbursed.

news.headline: Mulvihill Canadian Bank Enhanced Yield ETF's net assets grow to $122.66 million, $27.25 million increase in 2024. Distributions: $9.65 million.
news.summary: On March 28, 2025, the Mulvihill Canadian Bank Enhanced Yield ETF (TSX: CBNK) released its financial results for the year ending December 31, 2024. The net assets attributable to unit holders saw an increase of $27.25 million, equivalent to $2.00 per unit. As of December 31, 2024, the net assets attributable to unit holders totaled $122.66 million, which translates to $8.43 per unit. Throughout the year, cash distributions to unitholders reached $9.65 million, or $0.70 per unit.

news.headline: Largo's 2024 financial results announced, with plans for operational turnaround and cost optimization.
news.summary: Toronto-based company Largo announced their financial results for the fourth quarter and full year of 2024. The company revealed a strategic operational turnaround plan and introduced further cost optimization initiatives. This move comes in the wake of challenges faced in the previous year. Largo aims to strengthen its position in the market as it reported significant numbers for the mentioned period. The company's focus on green steel and sustainability will be central to its future endeavors.

news.headline: EpicCyber partners with TD SYNNEX to offer Zscaler services to Value-Add Resellers, enhancing enterprise solutions.
news.summary: A partnership has been announced between EpicCyber, a prominent provider of Zscaler deployment and operational services based in Mill Valley, California, and TD SYNNEX. The collaboration aims to bring enterprise-level Zscaler solutions and services to the Value-Add Resellers within their network. The partnership was established on March 28, 2025, and is designed to enhance the capabilities of the Value-Add Resellers by leveraging the expertise of EpicCyber in Zscaler technologies.

news.headline: Brazil to auction up to 20,000 foreign exchange swaps March 31
SEC drops investigation into Solana meme coin Hawk Tuah, says girl on TMZ.
news.summary: A new report in the field of technology reveals that global spending on cybersecurity products and services is expected to reach $174 billion by the end of this year. This marks an increase of 12% from the previous year, highlighting the growing importance of cybersecurity in today's digital world. The report also indicates that organizations are prioritizing investment in areas such as security operations, identity access management, and vulnerability assessment. With cyber threats becoming more sophisticated, companies are ramping up their efforts to protect their data and systems. The Asia-Pacific region is projected to see the highest growth in cybersecurity spending, followed by North America and Europe. Experts emphasize the need for businesses to stay vigilant and proactive in safeguarding their assets against cyber attacks.

news.headline: Appeals court overturns Dogecoin limits at aid agency amid Musk dispute.
Girl associated with Hawk Tuah claims SEC will cease investigation on Meme Coin.
news.summary: Ten million people in the United States have filed for unemployment benefits in the last two weeks, marking a record-breaking surge in job losses due to the coronavirus pandemic. The sharp increase in unemployment claims is fueling concerns about the economic impact of the crisis, with businesses forced to close and lay off workers to comply with social distancing measures. As the number of cases continues to rise across the country, analysts predict that the situation is only going to worsen in the coming weeks. The government is implementing stimulus packages to alleviate the financial strain on individuals and businesses affected by the pandemic, but the long-term effects on the economy remain uncertain.

news.headline: A review praises the GoBoat Fish & Double Fish for a game-changing fishing experience. Tulsa, Okla., March 28, 2025.
news.summary: In Tulsa, Oklahoma on March 28, 2025, an individual shared their excitement about their experience with the GoBoat Fish and GoBoat Double Fish. These innovative watercraft have made a significant impact on the fishing community. Having tested various fishing boats, kayaks, and rafts in the past, the individual was impressed by the unique features of these new products. The GoBoat Fish and GoBoat Double Fish offer a revolutionary fishing experience, and their popularity is on the rise. The individual's firsthand account highlights the growing interest in these watercraft, with the potential for increased adoption among fishing enthusiasts.

news.headline: Crypto market cycle permanently shifted — Polygon founder
news.summary: The traditional four-year cycle in the crypto market is becoming less pronounced due to the maturation of crypto as an asset class and the involvement of institutional investors. The overall speculative activity in the market has decreased because of high interest rates in the United States and low liquidity conditions. However, Polygon co-founder Sandeep Nailwal believes that once interest rates are cut and the Trump administration stabilizes, speculative activity will rebound. Despite the expectation of 30-40% drawdowns between cycles, Nailwal anticipates that drawdowns will be less severe for Blue Chip crypto assets in the future. The disruption of the four-year cycle also stems from US President Donald Trump's pro-crypto policies, including the establishment of a Bitcoin strategic reserve, which has legitimized crypto for institutional investors. Factors such as the rise of ETFs and macroeconomic pressures are further altering market cycles by propping up digital asset prices and keeping capital tied to certain investment vehicles. These shifts suggest a changing landscape in the crypto market dynamics, where greater maturity and stability may be observed in the future.

news.headline: Greenbrier to report Q2 financial results on Apr 7, 2025, after market close. Webcast and call planned.
news.summary: Lake Oswego, Oregon - The Greenbrier Companies, Inc. disclosed that it is scheduled to release its financial results for the second fiscal quarter concluding on February 28, 2025, post-market on Monday, April 7, 2025. Trading under the symbol NYSE: GBX, the company invites shareholders and interested individuals to tune in for updates.

news.headline: Trump grants pardon to Bitcoin billionaire Arthur Hayes and BitMEX founders Benjamin Delo and Samuel Reed.
news.summary: Three co-founders of BitMEX, Arthur Hayes, Benjamin Delo, and Samuel Reed, have received presidential pardons from President Trump. The pardons were granted as reported, in a recent development.

news.headline: S&P: Kyrgyzstan rating B+; outlook stable
WF Holding Limited closed their IPO offering of 2,000,000 ordinary shares at $4.00 each, generating $8 million. Share on Nasdaq as "WFF."
news.summary: On March 28, 2025, WF Holding Limited, a Malaysia-based FRP products manufacturer, completed its initial public offering of 2,000,000 Ordinary Shares at $4.00 per share, resulting in total gross proceeds of $8 million. The Company's Ordinary Shares began trading on the Nasdaq Capital Market on March 27, 2025 as "WFF."

news.headline: ZA Miner offers free cloud mining for Bitcoin, Dogecoin, and Litecoin to earn passive income in 2025.
news.summary: ZA Miner is a cloud mining company based in Middlesex, UK, established in 2020. They offer services in Bitcoin, Dogecoin, and Litecoin mining without the need for expensive hardware investments. Their approach aligns with Donald Trump's pro-mining stance, focusing on economic empowerment and innovation in the crypto sector. ZA Miner leverages state-of-the-art mining facilities in energy-rich regions like Kazakhstan and Iceland. This enables users to earn passive income through mining while staying aligned with global trends and policies supporting cryptocurrency growth.
